I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
RangeError: Position out of range when deleting after multi-column block
### What’s broken?

When using the multi-column feature, continuously pressing Backspace after typing in a block following a multi-column layout causes a runtime error:

```RangeError: Position X out of range```

This crashes the editor and breaks further interaction.

<img width="1225" height="838" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/de3d5796-0967-40e8-bbd3-98c4147efaec" />

<img width="592" height="174" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ccd0b6d-4eed-46c0-8193-34692e7e225f" />

### What did you expect to happen?

- Text should be deleted normally
- Editor should remain stable
- No runtime errors should occur


### Steps to reproduce

1. Insert a multi-column block (e.g., 3 columns)
2. Move cursor to the next block (below the columns)
3. Type some text (e.g., aaa)
4. Press and hold Backspace to delete continuously

### BlockNote version

"@blocknote/xl-multi-column": "0.47.1"

### Environment

Chrome

### Additional context

- This issue appears to be related to how the multi-column structure interacts with deletion logic.
- Likely caused by an invalid ProseMirror transaction when deleting across structural boundaries.
- Happens more frequently when holding down Backspace (rapid consecutive deletes).

### Contribution

- [ ] I'd be interested in contributing a fix for this issue

### Sponsor

- [ ] I'm a [sponsor](https://www.blocknotejs.org/pricing) and would appreciate if you could look into this sooner than later 💖

</pr_description>

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/packages/xl-multi-column/src/pm-nodes/Column.ts:
     1	import { Node } from "@tiptap/core";
     2	
     3	import { createColumnResizeExtension } from "../extensions/ColumnResize/ColumnResizeExtension.js";
     4	
     5	export const Column = Node.create({
     6	  name: "column",
     7	  group: "bnBlock childContainer",
     8	  // A block always contains content, and optionally a blockGroup which contains nested blocks
     9	  content: "blockContainer+",
    10	  priority: 40,
    11	  defining: true,
    12	  marks: "deletion insertion modification",
    13	  addAttributes() {
    14	    return {
    15	      width: {
    16	        // Why does each column have a default width of 1, i.e. 100%? Because
    17	        // when creating a new column, we want to make sure that existing
    18	        // column widths are preserved, while the new one also has a sensible
    19	        // width. If we'd set it so all column widths must add up to 100%
    20	        // instead, then each time a new column is created, we'd have to assign
    21	        // it a width depending on the total number of columns and also adjust
    22	        // the widths of the other columns. The same can be said for using px
    23	        // instead of percent widths and making them add to the editor width. So
    24	        // using this method is both simpler and computationally cheaper. This
    25	        // is possible because we can set the `flex-grow` property to the width
    26	        // value, which handles all the resizing for us, instead of manually
    27	        // having to set the `width` property of each column.
    28	        default: 1,
    29	        parseHTML: (element) => {
    30	          const attr = element.getAttribute("data-width");
    31	          if (attr === null) {
    32	            return null;
    33	          }
    34	
    35	          const parsed = parseFloat(attr);
    36	          if (isFinite(parsed)) {
    37	            return parsed;
    38	          }
    39	
    40	          return null;
    41	        },
    42	        renderHTML: (attributes) => {
    43	          return {
    44	            "data-width": (attributes.width as number).toString(),
    45	            style: `flex-grow: ${attributes.width as number};`,
    46	          };
    47	        },
    48	      },
    49	    };
    50	  },
    51	
    52	  parseHTML() {
    53	    return [
    54	      {
    55	        tag: "div",
    56	        getAttrs: (element) => {
    57	          if (typeof element === "string") {
    58	            return false;
    59	          }
    60	
    61	          if (element.getAttribute("data-node-type") === this.name) {
    62	            return {};
    63	          }
    64	
    65	          return false;
    66	        },
    67	      },
    68	    ];
    69	  },
    70	
    71	  renderHTML({ HTMLAttributes }) {
    72	    const column = document.createElement("div");
    73	    column.className = "bn-block-column";
    74	    column.setAttribute("data-node-type", this.name);
    75	    for (const [attribute, value] of Object.entries(HTMLAttributes)) {
    76	      column.setAttribute(attribute, value as any); // TODO as any
    77	    }
    78	
    79	    return {
    80	      dom: column,
    81	      contentDOM: column,
    82	    };
    83	  },
    84	
    85	  addExtensions() {
    86	    return [createColumnResizeExtension(this.options.editor)];
    87	  },
    88	});
    89

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/packages/core/src/api/blockManipulation/commands/replaceBlocks/util/fixColumnList.ts:
     1	import { Slice, type Node } from "prosemirror-model";
     2	import { type Transaction } from "prosemirror-state";
     3	import { ReplaceAroundStep } from "prosemirror-transform";
     4	
     5	/**
     6	 * Checks if a `column` node is empty, i.e. if it has only a single empty
     7	 * paragraph.
     8	 * @param column The column to check.
     9	 * @returns Whether the column is empty.
    10	 */
    11	export function isEmptyColumn(column: Node) {
    12	  if (!column || column.type.name !== "column") {
    13	    throw new Error("Invalid columnPos: does not point to column node.");
    14	  }
    15	
    16	  const blockContainer = column.firstChild;
    17	  if (!blockContainer) {
    18	    throw new Error("Invalid column: does not have child node.");
    19	  }
    20	
    21	  const blockContent = blockContainer.firstChild;
    22	  if (!blockContent) {
    23	    throw new Error("Invalid blockContainer: does not have child node.");
    24	  }
    25	
    26	  return (
    27	    column.childCount === 1 &&
    28	    blockContainer.childCount === 1 &&
    29	    blockContent.type.name === "paragraph" &&
    30	    blockContent.content.content.length === 0
    31	  );
    32	}
    33	
    34	/**
    35	 * Removes all empty `column` nodes in a `columnList`. A `column` node is empty
    36	 * if it has only a single empty block. If, however, removing the `column`s
    37	 * leaves the `columnList` that has fewer than two, ProseMirror will re-add
    38	 * empty columns.
    39	 * @param tr The `Transaction` to add the changes to.
    40	 * @param columnListPos The position just before the `columnList` node.
    41	 */
    42	export function removeEmptyColumns(tr: Transaction, columnListPos: number) {
    43	  const $columnListPos = tr.doc.resolve(columnListPos);
    44	  const columnList = $columnListPos.nodeAfter;
    45	  if (!columnList || columnList.type.name !== "columnList") {
    46	    throw new Error(
    47	      "Invalid columnListPos: does not point to columnList node.",
    48	    );
    49	  }
    50	
    51	  for (
    52	    let columnIndex = columnList.childCount - 1;
    53	    columnIndex >= 0;
    54	    columnIndex--
    55	  ) {
    56	    const columnPos = tr.doc
    57	      .resolve($columnListPos.pos + 1)
    58	      .posAtIndex(columnIndex);
    59	    const $columnPos = tr.doc.resolve(columnPos);
    60	    const column = $columnPos.nodeAfter;
    61	    if (!column || column.type.name !== "column") {
    62	      throw new Error("Invalid columnPos: does not point to column node.");
    63	    }
    64	
    65	    if (isEmptyColumn(column)) {
    66	      tr.delete(columnPos, columnPos + column.nodeSize);
    67	    }
    68	  }
    69	}
    70	
    71	/**
    72	 * Fixes potential issues in a `columnList` node after a
    73	 * `blockContainer`/`column` node is (re)moved from it:
    74	 *
    75	 * - Removes all empty `column` nodes. A `column` node is empty if it has only
    76	 * a single empty block.
    77	 * - If all but one `column` nodes are empty, replaces the `columnList` with
    78	 * the content of the non-empty `column`.
    79	 * - If all `column` nodes are empty, removes the `columnList` entirely.
    80	 * @param tr The `Transaction` to add the changes to.
    81	 * @param columnListPos
    82	 * @returns The position just before the `columnList` node.
    83	 */
    84	export function fixColumnList(tr: Transaction, columnListPos: number) {
    85	  removeEmptyColumns(tr, columnListPos);
    86	
    87	  const $columnListPos = tr.doc.resolve(columnListPos);
    88	  const columnList = $columnListPos.nodeAfter;
    89	  if (!columnList || columnList.type.name !== "columnList") {
    90	    throw new Error(
    91	      "Invalid columnListPos: does not point to columnList node.",
    92	    );
    93	  }
    94	
    95	  if (columnList.childCount > 2) {
    96	    // Do nothing if the `columnList` has more than two non-empty `column`s. In
    97	    // the case that the `columnList` has exactly two columns, we may need to
    98	    // still remove it, as it's possible that one or both columns are empty.
    99	    // This is because after `removeEmptyColumns` is called, if the
   100	    // `columnList` has fewer than two `column`s, ProseMirror will re-add empty
   101	    // `column`s until there are two total, in order to fit the schema.
   102	    return;
   103	  }
   104	
   105	  if (columnList.childCount < 2) {
   106	    // Throw an error if the `columnList` has fewer than two columns. After
   107	    // `removeEmptyColumns` is called, if the `columnList` has fewer than two
   108	    // `column`s, ProseMirror will re-add empty `column`s until there are two
   109	    // total, in order to fit the schema. So if there are fewer than two here,
   110	    // either the schema, or ProseMirror's internals, must have changed.
   111	    throw new Error("Invalid columnList: contains fewer than two children.");
   112	  }
   113	
   114	  const firstColumnBeforePos = columnListPos + 1;
   115	  const $firstColumnBeforePos = tr.doc.resolve(firstColumnBeforePos);
   116	  const firstColumn = $firstColumnBeforePos.nodeAfter;
   117	
   118	  const lastColumnAfterPos = columnListPos + columnList.nodeSize - 1;
   119	  const $lastColumnAfterPos = tr.doc.resolve(lastColumnAfterPos);
   120	  const lastColumn = $lastColumnAfterPos.nodeBefore;
   121	
   122	  if (!firstColumn || !lastColumn) {
   123	    throw new Error("Invalid columnList: does not contain children.");
   124	  }
   125	
   126	  const firstColumnEmpty = isEmptyColumn(firstColumn);
   127	  const lastColumnEmpty = isEmptyColumn(lastColumn);
   128	
   129	  if (firstColumnEmpty && lastColumnEmpty) {
   130	    // Removes `columnList`
   131	    tr.delete(columnListPos, columnListPos + columnList.nodeSize);
   132	
   133	    return;
   134	  }
   135	
   136	  if (firstColumnEmpty) {
   137	    tr.step(
   138	      new ReplaceAroundStep(
   139	        // Replaces `columnList`.
   140	        columnListPos,
   141	        columnListPos + columnList.nodeSize,
   142	        // Replaces with content of last `column`.
   143	        lastColumnAfterPos - lastColumn.nodeSize + 1,
   144	        lastColumnAfterPos - 1,
   145	        // Doesn't append anything.
   146	        Slice.empty,
   147	        0,
   148	        false,
   149	      ),
   150	    );
   151	
   152	    return;
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  if (lastColumnEmpty) {
   156	    tr.step(
   157	      new ReplaceAroundStep(
   158	        // Replaces `columnList`.
   159	        columnListPos,
   160	        columnListPos + columnList.nodeSize,
   161	        // Replaces with content of first `column`.
   162	        firstColumnBeforePos + 1,
   163	        firstColumnBeforePos + firstColumn.nodeSize - 1,
   164	        // Doesn't append anything.
   165	        Slice.empty,
   166	        0,
   167	        false,
   168	      ),
   169	    );
   170	
   171	    return;
   172	  }
   173	}
   174

    35	
    36	export class BlockManager<
    37	  BSchema extends BlockSchema = DefaultBlockSchema,
    38	  ISchema extends InlineContentSchema = DefaultInlineContentSchema,
    39	  SSchema extends StyleSchema = DefaultStyleSchema,
    40	> {
    41	  constructor(private editor: BlockNoteEditor<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>) {}
    42	
    43	  /**
    44	   * Gets a snapshot of all top-level (non-nested) blocks in the editor.
    45	   * @returns A snapshot of all top-level (non-nested) blocks in the editor.
    46	   */
    47	  public get document(): Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>[] {
    48	    return this.editor.transact((tr) => {
    49	      return docToBlocks(tr.doc, this.editor.pmSchema);
    50	    });
    51	  }
    52	
    53	  /**
    54	   * Gets a snapshot of an existing block from the editor.
    55	   * @param blockIdentifier The identifier of an existing block that should be
    56	   * retrieved.
    57	   * @returns The block that matches the identifier, or `undefined` if no
    58	   * matching block was found.
    59	   */
    60	  public getBlock(
    61	    blockIdentifier: BlockIdentifier,
    62	  ): Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema> | undefined {
    63	    return this.editor.transact((tr) => getBlock(tr.doc, blockIdentifier));
    64	  }
    65	
    66	  /**
    67	   * Gets a snapshot of the previous sibling of an existing block from the
    68	   * editor.
    69	   * @param blockIdentifier The identifier of an existing block for which the
    70	   * previous sibling should be retrieved.
    71	   * @returns The previous sibling of the block that matches the identifier.
    72	   * `undefined` if no matching block was found, or it's the first child/block
    73	   * in the document.
    74	   */
    75	  public getPrevBlock(
    76	    blockIdentifier: BlockIdentifier,
    77	  ): Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema> | undefined {
    78	    return this.editor.transact((tr) => getPrevBlock(tr.doc, blockIdentifier));
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  /**
    82	   * Gets a snapshot of the next sibling of an existing block from the editor.
    83	   * @param blockIdentifier The identifier of an existing block for which the
    84	   * next sibling should be retrieved.
    85	   * @returns The next sibling of the block that matches the identifier.
    86	   * `undefined` if no matching block was found, or it's the last child/block in
    87	   * the document.
    88	   */
    89	  public getNextBlock(
    90	    blockIdentifier: BlockIdentifier,
    91	  ): Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema> | undefined {
    92	    return this.editor.transact((tr) => getNextBlock(tr.doc, blockIdentifier));
    93	  }
    94	
    95	  /**
    96	   * Gets a snapshot of the parent of an existing block from the editor.
    97	   * @param blockIdentifier The identifier of an existing block for which the
    98	   * parent should be retrieved.
    99	   * @returns The parent of the block that matches the identifier. `undefined`
   100	   * if no matching block was found, or the block isn't nested.
   101	   */
   102	  public getParentBlock(
   103	    blockIdentifier: BlockIdentifier,
   104	  ): Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema> | undefined {
   105	    return this.editor.transact((tr) =>
   106	      getParentBlock(tr.doc, blockIdentifier),
   107	    );
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  /**
   111	   * Traverses all blocks in the editor depth-first, and executes a callback for each.
   112	   * @param callback The callback to execute for each block. Returning `false` stops the traversal.
   113	   * @param reverse Whether the blocks should be traversed in reverse order.
   114	   */
   115	  public forEachBlock(
   116	    callback: (block: Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>) => boolean,
   117	    reverse = false,
   118	  ): void {
   119	    const blocks = this.document.slice();
   120	
   121	    if (reverse) {
   122	      blocks.reverse();
   123	    }
   124	
   125	    function traverseBlockArray(
   126	      blockArray: Block<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>[],
   127	    ): boolean {
   128	      for (const block of blockArray) {
   129	        if (callback(block) === false) {
   130	          return false;
   131	        }
   132	
   133	        const children = reverse
   134	          ? block.children.slice().reverse()
   135	          : block.children;
   136	
   137	        if (!traverseBlockArray(children)) {
   138	          return false;
   139	        }
   140	      }
   141	
   142	      return true;
   143	    }
   144	
   145	    traverseBlockArray(blocks);
   146	  }
   147	
   148	  /**
   149	   * Inserts new blocks into the editor. If a block's `id` is undefined, BlockNote generates one automatically. Throws an
   150	   * error if the reference block could not be found.
   151	   * @param blocksToInsert An array of partial blocks that should be inserted.
   152	   * @param referenceBlock An identifier for an existing block, at which the new blocks should be inserted.
   153	   * @param placement Whether the blocks should be inserted just before, just after, or nested inside the
   154	   * `referenceBlock`.
   155	   */
   156	  public insertBlocks(
   157	    blocksToInsert: PartialBlock<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>[],
   158	    referenceBlock: BlockIdentifier,
   159	    placement: "before" | "after" = "before",
   160	  ) {
   161	    return this.editor.transact((tr) =>
   162	      insertBlocks(tr, blocksToInsert, referenceBlock, placement),
   163	    );
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  /**
   167	   * Updates an existing block in the editor. Since updatedBlock is a PartialBlock object, some fields might not be
   168	   * defined. These undefined fields are kept as-is from the existing block. Throws an error if the block to update could
   169	   * not be found.
   170	   * @param blockToUpdate The block that should be updated.
   171	   * @param update A partial block which defines how the existing block should be changed.
   172	   */
   173	  public updateBlock(
   174	    blockToUpdate: BlockIdentifier,
   175	    update: PartialBlock<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>,
   176	  ) {
   177	    return this.editor.transact((tr) => updateBlock(tr, blockToUpdate, update));
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  /**
   181	   * Removes existing blocks from the editor. Throws an error if any of the blocks could not be found.
   182	   * @param blocksToRemove An array of identifiers for existing blocks that should be removed.
   183	   */
   184	  public removeBlocks(blocksToRemove: BlockIdentifier[]) {
   185	    return this.editor.transact(
   186	      (tr) => removeAndInsertBlocks(tr, blocksToRemove, []).removedBlocks,
   187	    );
   188	  }
   189	
   190	  /**
   191	   * Replaces existing blocks in the editor with new blocks. If the blocks that should be removed are not adjacent or
   192	   * are at different nesting levels, `blocksToInsert` will be inserted at the position of the first block in
   193	   * `blocksToRemove`. Throws an error if any of the blocks to remove could not be found.
   194	   * @param blocksToRemove An array of blocks that should be replaced.
   195	   * @param blocksToInsert An array of partial blocks to replace the old ones with.
   196	   */
   197	  public replaceBlocks(
   198	    blocksToRemove: BlockIdentifier[],
   199	    blocksToInsert: PartialBlock<BSchema, ISchema, SSchema>[],
   200	  ) {
   201	    return this.editor.transact((tr) =>
   202	      removeAndInsertBlocks(tr, blocksToRemove, blocksToInsert),
   203	    );
   204	  }
   205	
   206	  /**
   207	   * Checks if the block containing the text cursor can be nested.
   208	   */
   209	  public canNestBlock() {
   210	    return canNestBlock(this.editor);
   211	  }
   212	
   213	  /**
   214	   * Nests the block containing the text cursor into the block above it.
   215	   */
   216	  public nestBlock() {
   217	    nestBlock(this.editor);
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  /**
   221	   * Checks if the block containing the text cursor is nested.
   222	   */
   223	  public canUnnestBlock() {
   224	    return canUnnestBlock(this.editor);
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  /**
   228	   * Lifts the block containing the text cursor out of its parent.
   229	   */
   230	  public unnestBlock() {
   231	    unnestBlock(this.editor);
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  /**
   235	   * Moves the selected blocks up. If the previous block has children, moves
   236	   * them to the end of its children. If there is no previous block, but the
   237	   * current blocks share a common parent, moves them out of & before it.
   238	   */
   239	  public moveBlocksUp() {
   240	    return moveBlocksUp(this.editor);
   241	  }
   242	
   243	  /**
   244	   * Moves the selected blocks down. If the next block has children, moves
   245	   * them to the start of its children. If there is no next block, but the
   246	   * current blocks share a common parent, moves them out of & after it.
   247	   */
   248	  public moveBlocksDown() {
   249	    return moveBlocksDown(this.editor);
   250	  }
   251	}
   252

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/packages/core/src/api/blockManipulation/commands/replaceBlocks/replaceBlocks.ts:
     1	import { type Node } from "prosemirror-model";
     2	import { type Transaction } from "prosemirror-state";
     3	import type { Block, PartialBlock } from "../../../../blocks/defaultBlocks.js";
     4	import type {
     5	  BlockIdentifier,
     6	  BlockSchema,
     7	  InlineContentSchema,
     8	  StyleSchema,
     9	} from "../../../../schema/index.js";
    10	import { blockToNode } from "../../../nodeConversions/blockToNode.js";
    11	import { nodeToBlock } from "../../../nodeConversions/nodeToBlock.js";
    12	import { getPmSchema } from "../../../pmUtil.js";
    13	import { fixColumnList } from "./util/fixColumnList.js";
    14	
    15	export function removeAndInsertBlocks<
    16	  BSchema extends BlockSchema,
    17	  I extends InlineContentSchema,
    18	  S extends StyleSchema,
    19	>(
    20	  tr: Transaction,
    21	  blocksToRemove: BlockIdentifier[],
    22	  blocksToInsert: PartialBlock<BSchema, I, S>[],
    23	): {
    24	  insertedBlocks: Block<BSchema, I, S>[];
    25	  removedBlocks: Block<BSchema, I, S>[];
    26	} {
    27	  const pmSchema = getPmSchema(tr);
    28	  // Converts the `PartialBlock`s to ProseMirror nodes to insert them into the
    29	  // document.
    30	  const nodesToInsert: Node[] = blocksToInsert.map((block) =>
    31	    blockToNode(block, pmSchema),
    32	  );
    33	
    34	  const idsOfBlocksToRemove = new Set<string>(
    35	    blocksToRemove.map((block) =>
    36	      typeof block === "string" ? block : block.id,
    37	    ),
    38	  );
    39	  const removedBlocks: Block<BSchema, I, S>[] = [];
    40	  const columnListPositions = new Set<number>();
    41	
    42	  const idOfFirstBlock =
    43	    typeof blocksToRemove[0] === "string"
    44	      ? blocksToRemove[0]
    45	      : blocksToRemove[0].id;
    46	  let removedSize = 0;
    47	
    48	  tr.doc.descendants((node, pos) => {
    49	    // Skips traversing nodes after all target blocks have been removed.
    50	    if (idsOfBlocksToRemove.size === 0) {
    51	      return false;
    52	    }
    53	
    54	    // Keeps traversing nodes if block with target ID has not been found.
    55	    if (
    56	      !node.type.isInGroup("bnBlock") ||
    57	      !idsOfBlocksToRemove.has(node.attrs.id)
    58	    ) {
    59	      return true;
    60	    }
    61	
    62	    // Saves the block that is being deleted.
    63	    removedBlocks.push(nodeToBlock(node, pmSchema));
    64	    idsOfBlocksToRemove.delete(node.attrs.id);
    65	
    66	    if (blocksToInsert.length > 0 && node.attrs.id === idOfFirstBlock) {
    67	      const oldDocSize = tr.doc.nodeSize;
    68	      tr.insert(pos, nodesToInsert);
    69	      const newDocSize = tr.doc.nodeSize;
    70	
    71	      removedSize += oldDocSize - newDocSize;
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    const oldDocSize = tr.doc.nodeSize;
    75	
    76	    const $pos = tr.doc.resolve(pos - removedSize);
    77	
    78	    if ($pos.node().type.name === "column") {
    79	      columnListPositions.add($pos.before(-1));
    80	    } else if ($pos.node().type.name === "columnList") {
    81	      columnListPositions.add($pos.before());
    82	    }
    83	
    84	    if (
    85	      $pos.node().type.name === "blockGroup" &&
    86	      $pos.node($pos.depth - 1).type.name !== "doc" &&
    87	      $pos.node().childCount === 1
    88	    ) {
    89	      // Checks if the block is the only child of a parent `blockGroup` node.
    90	      // In this case, we need to delete the parent `blockGroup` node instead
    91	      // of just the `blockContainer`.
    92	      tr.delete($pos.before(), $pos.after());
    93	    } else {
    94	      tr.delete(pos - removedSize, pos - removedSize + node.nodeSize);
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    const newDocSize = tr.doc.nodeSize;
    98	    removedSize += oldDocSize - newDocSize;
    99	
   100	    return false;
   101	  });
   102	
   103	  // Throws an error if not all blocks could be found.
   104	  if (idsOfBlocksToRemove.size > 0) {
   105	    const notFoundIds = [...idsOfBlocksToRemove].join("\n");
   106	
   107	    throw Error(
   108	      "Blocks with the following IDs could not be found in the editor: " +
   109	        notFoundIds,
   110	    );
   111	  }
   112	
   113	  columnListPositions.forEach((pos) => fixColumnList(tr, pos));
   114	
   115	  // Converts the nodes created from `blocksToInsert` into full `Block`s.
   116	  const insertedBlocks = nodesToInsert.map((node) =>
   117	    nodeToBlock(node, pmSchema),
   118	  ) as Block<BSchema, I, S>[];
   119	
   120	  return { insertedBlocks, removedBlocks };
   121	}
   122

Now I see the issue! In `replaceBlocks.ts`, when collecting `columnListPositions`, it checks for `$pos.node().type.name === "column"` or `$pos.node().type.name === "columnList"`. Then it adds positions to a set. BUT the issue is that after multiple deletions, the positions may no longer be valid because the document has changed.

The real issue is that when you delete blocks, the `fixColumnList` function is called on saved positions. But if multiple blocks are being deleted from within the same columnList, the position stored might become invalid after previous deletions.

Let me think about this more carefully. The issue described is "Position out of range when deleting after multi-column block". That means when you press backspace and delete from a block AFTER a multi-column block, something goes wrong.

Let me look at how backspace triggers deletion. It likely goes through the keyboard shortcuts or backspace handling logic

    23	
    24	export const KeyboardShortcutsExtension = Extension.create<{
    25	  editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>;
    26	  tabBehavior: "prefer-navigate-ui" | "prefer-indent";
    27	}>({
    28	  priority: 50,
    29	
    30	  // TODO: The shortcuts need a refactor. Do we want to use a command priority
    31	  //  design as there is now, or clump the logic into a single function?
    32	  addKeyboardShortcuts() {
    33	    // handleBackspace is partially adapted from https://github.com/ueberdosis/tiptap/blob/ed56337470efb4fd277128ab7ef792b37cfae992/packages/core/src/extensions/keymap.ts
    34	    const handleBackspace = () =>
    35	      this.editor.commands.first(({ chain, commands }) => [
    36	        // Deletes the selection if it's not empty.
    37	        () => commands.deleteSelection(),
    38	        // Undoes an input rule if one was triggered in the last editor state change.
    39	        () => commands.undoInputRule(),
    40	        // Reverts block content type to a paragraph if the selection is at the start of the block.
    41	        () =>
    42	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
    43	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
    44	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
    45	              return false;
    46	            }
    47	
    48	            const selectionAtBlockStart =
    49	              state.selection.from === blockInfo.blockContent.beforePos + 1;
    50	            const isParagraph =
    51	              blockInfo.blockContent.node.type.name === "paragraph";
    52	
    53	            if (selectionAtBlockStart && !isParagraph) {
    54	              return commands.command(
    55	                updateBlockCommand(blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos, {
    56	                  type: "paragraph",
    57	                  props: {},
    58	                }),
    59	              );
    60	            }
    61	
    62	            return false;
    63	          }),
    64	        // Removes a level of nesting if the block is indented if the selection is at the start of the block.
    65	        () =>
    66	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
    67	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
    68	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
    69	              return false;
    70	            }
    71	            const { blockContent } = blockInfo;
    72	
    73	            const selectionAtBlockStart =
    74	              state.selection.from === blockContent.beforePos + 1;
    75	
    76	            if (selectionAtBlockStart) {
    77	              return commands.liftListItem("blockContainer");
    78	            }
    79	
    80	            return false;
    81	          }),
    82	        // Merges block with the previous one if it isn't indented, and the selection is at the start of the
    83	        // block. The target block for merging must contain inline content.
    84	        () =>
    85	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
    86	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
    87	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
    88	              return false;
    89	            }
    90	            const { bnBlock: blockContainer, blockContent } = blockInfo;
    91	
    92	            const prevBlockInfo = getPrevBlockInfo(
    93	              state.doc,
    94	              blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
    95	            );
    96	            // If the previous block has no inline content, it can't be merged.
    97	            // It's instead deleted, which is done later in the chan, so we
    98	            // return early here.
    99	            if (
   100	              !prevBlockInfo ||
   101	              !prevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer ||
   102	              prevBlockInfo.blockContent.node.type.spec.content !== "inline*"
   103	            ) {
   104	              return false;
   105	            }
   106	
   107	            const selectionAtBlockStart =
   108	              state.selection.from === blockContent.beforePos + 1;
   109	            const selectionEmpty = state.selection.empty;
   110	
   111	            const posBetweenBlocks = blockContainer.beforePos;
   112	
   113	            if (selectionAtBlockStart && selectionEmpty) {
   114	              return chain()
   115	                .command(mergeBlocksCommand(posBetweenBlocks))
   116	                .scrollIntoView()
   117	                .run();
   118	            }
   119	
   120	            return false;
   121	          }),
   122	        // If the previous block is a columnList, moves the current block to
   123	        // the end of the last column in it.
   124	        () =>
   125	          commands.command(({ state, tr, dispatch }) => {
   126	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
   127	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   128	              return false;
   129	            }
   130	
   131	            const prevBlockInfo = getPrevBlockInfo(
   132	              state.doc,
   133	              blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   134	            );
   135	            if (!prevBlockInfo || prevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   136	              return false;
   137	            }
   138	
   139	            if (dispatch) {
   140	              const columnAfterPos = prevBlockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos - 1;
   141	              const $blockAfterPos = tr.doc.resolve(columnAfterPos - 1);
   142	
   143	              tr.delete(
   144	                blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   145	                blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   146	              );
   147	              tr.insert($blockAfterPos.pos, blockInfo.bnBlock.node);
   148	              tr.setSelection(
   149	                TextSelection.near(tr.doc.resolve($blockAfterPos.pos + 1)),
   150	              );
   151	
   152	              return true;
   153	            }
   154	
   155	            return false;
   156	          }),
   157	        // If the block is the first in a column, moves it to the end of the
   158	        // previous column. If there is no previous column, moves it above the
   159	        // columnList.
   160	        () =>
   161	          commands.command(({ state, tr, dispatch }) => {
   162	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
   163	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   164	              return false;
   165	            }
   166	
   167	            const selectionAtBlockStart =
   168	              tr.selection.from === blockInfo.blockContent.beforePos + 1;
   169	            if (!selectionAtBlockStart) {
   170	              return false;
   171	            }
   172	
   173	            const $pos = tr.doc.resolve(blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos);
   174	
   175	            const prevBlock = $pos.nodeBefore;
   176	            if (prevBlock) {
   177	              return false;
   178	            }
   179	
   180	            const parentBlock = $pos.node();
   181	            if (parentBlock.type.name !== "column") {
   182	              return false;
   183	            }
   184	
   185	            const $blockPos = tr.doc.resolve(blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos);
   186	            const $columnPos = tr.doc.resolve($blockPos.before());
   187	            const columnListPos = $columnPos.before();
   188	
   189	            if (dispatch) {
   190	              tr.delete(
   191	                blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   192	                blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   193	              );
   194	              fixColumnList(tr, columnListPos);
   195	
   196	              if ($columnPos.pos === columnListPos + 1) {
   197	                tr.insert(columnListPos, blockInfo.bnBlock.node);
   198	                tr.setSelection(
   199	                  TextSelection.near(tr.doc.resolve(columnListPos)),
   200	                );
   201	              } else {
   202	                tr.insert($columnPos.pos - 1, blockInfo.bnBlock.node);
   203	                tr.setSelection(
   204	                  TextSelection.near(tr.doc.resolve($columnPos.pos)),
   205	                );
   206	              }
   207	            }
   208	
   209	            return true;
   210	          }),
   211	        // Deletes the current block if it's an empty block with inline content,
   212	        // and moves the selection to the previous block.
   213	        () =>
   214	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
   215	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
   216	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   217	              return false;
   218	            }
   219	
   220	            const blockEmpty =
   221	              blockInfo.blockContent.node.childCount === 0 &&
   222	              blockInfo.blockContent.node.type.spec.content === "inline*";
   223	
   224	            if (blockEmpty) {
   225	              const prevBlockInfo = getPrevBlockInfo(
   226	                state.doc,
   227	                blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   228	              );
   229	              if (!prevBlockInfo) {
   230	                return false;
   231	              }
   232	              const bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo = getBottomNestedBlockInfo(
   233	                state.doc,
   234	                prevBlockInfo,
   235	              );
   236	              if (!bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   237	                return false;
   238	              }
   239	              if (
   240	                !bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo ||
   241	                !bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer
   242	              ) {
   243	                return false;
   244	              }
   245	
   246	              let chainedCommands = chain();
   247	
   248	              // Moves the children the current block.
   249	              if (blockInfo.childContainer) {
   250	                chainedCommands.insertContentAt(
   251	                  blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   252	                  blockInfo.childContainer?.node.content,
   253	                );
   254	              }
   255	
   256	              if (
   257	                bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo.blockContent.node.type.spec
   258	                  .content === "tableRow+"
   259	              ) {
   260	                const tableBlockEndPos = blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos - 1;
   261	                const tableBlockContentEndPos = tableBlockEndPos - 1;
   262	                const lastRowEndPos = tableBlockContentEndPos - 1;
   263	                const lastCellEndPos = lastRowEndPos - 1;
   264	                const lastCellParagraphEndPos = lastCellEndPos - 1;
   265	
   266	                chainedCommands = chainedCommands.setTextSelection(
   267	                  lastCellParagraphEndPos,
   268	                );
   269	              } else if (
   270	                bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo.blockContent.node.type.spec
   271	                  .content === ""
   272	              ) {
   273	                chainedCommands = chainedCommands.setNodeSelection(
   274	                  bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo.blockContent.beforePos,
   275	                );
   276	              } else {
   277	                const blockContentEndPos =
   278	                  bottomNestedPrevBlockInfo.blockContent.afterPos - 1;
   279	
   280	                chainedCommands =
   281	                  chainedCommands.setTextSelection(blockContentEndPos);
   282	              }
   283	
   284	              return chainedCommands
   285	                .deleteRange({
   286	                  from: blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   287	                  to: blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   288	                })
   289	                .scrollIntoView()
   290	                .run();
   291	            }
   292	
   293	            return false;
   294	          }),
   295	        // Deletes previous block if it contains no content and isn't a table,
   296	        // when the selection is empty and at the start of the block. Moves the
   297	        // current block into the deleted block's place.
   298	        () =>
   299	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
   300	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
   301	
   302	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   303	              return false;
   304	            }
   305	
   306	            const selectionAtBlockStart =
   307	              state.selection.from === blockInfo.blockContent.beforePos + 1;
   308	            const selectionEmpty = state.selection.empty;
   309	
   310	            const prevBlockInfo = getPrevBlockInfo(
   311	              state.doc,
   312	              blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   313	            );
   314	
   315	            if (prevBlockInfo && selectionAtBlockStart && selectionEmpty) {
   316	              const bottomBlock = getBottomNestedBlockInfo(
   317	                state.doc,
   318	                prevBlockInfo,
   319	              );
   320	
   321	              if (!bottomBlock.isBlockContainer) {
   322	                return false;
   323	              }
   324	
   325	              const prevBlockNotTableAndNoContent =
   326	                bottomBlock.blockContent.node.type.spec.content === "" ||
   327	                (bottomBlock.blockContent.node.type.spec.content ===
   328	                  "inline*" &&
   329	                  bottomBlock.blockContent.node.childCount === 0);
   330	
   331	              if (prevBlockNotTableAndNoContent) {
   332	                return chain()
   333	                  .cut(
   334	                    {
   335	                      from: blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   336	                      to: blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   337	                    },
   338	                    bottomBlock.bnBlock.afterPos,
   339	                  )
   340	                  .deleteRange({
   341	                    from: bottomBlock.bnBlock.beforePos,
   342	                    to: bottomBlock.bnBlock.afterPos,
   343	                  })
   344	                  .run();
   345	              }
   346	            }
   347	
   348	            return false;
   349	          }),
   350	      ]);
   351	
   352	    const handleDelete = () =>
   353	      this.editor.commands.first(({ chain, commands }) => [
   354	        // Deletes the selection if it's not empty.
   355	        () => commands.deleteSelection(),
   356	        // Deletes the first child block and un-nests its children, if the
   357	        // selection is empty and at the end of the current block. If both the
   358	        // parent and child blocks have inline content, the child block's
   359	        // content is appended to the parent's. The child block's own children
   360	        // are unindented before it's deleted.
   361	        () =>
   362	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
   363	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
   364	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer || !blockInfo.childContainer) {
   365	              return false;
   366	            }
   367	            const { blockContent, childContainer } = blockInfo;
   368	
   369	            const selectionAtBlockEnd =
   370	              state.selection.from === blockContent.afterPos - 1;
   371	            const selectionEmpty = state.selection.empty;
   372	
   373	            const firstChildBlockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos(
   374	              state.doc.resolve(childContainer.beforePos + 1),
   375	            );
   376	            if (!firstChildBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   377	              return false;
   378	            }
   379	
   380	            if (selectionAtBlockEnd && selectionEmpty) {
   381	              const firstChildBlockContent =
   382	                firstChildBlockInfo.blockContent.node;
   383	              const firstChildBlockHasInlineContent =
   384	                firstChildBlockContent.type.spec.content === "inline*";
   385	              const blockHasInlineContent =
   386	                blockContent.node.type.spec.content === "inline*";
   387	
   388	              return (
   389	                chain()
   390	                  // Un-nests child block's children if necessary.
   391	                  .insertContentAt(
   392	                    firstChildBlockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   393	                    firstChildBlockInfo.childContainer?.node.content ||
   394	                      Fragment.empty,
   395	                  )
   396	                  .deleteRange(
   397	                    // Deletes whole child container if there's only one child.
   398	                    childContainer.node.childCount === 1
   399	                      ? {
   400	                          from: childContainer.beforePos,
   401	                          to: childContainer.afterPos,
   402	                        }
   403	                      : {
   404	                          from: firstChildBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   405	                          to: firstChildBlockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   406	                        },
   407	                  )
   408	                  // Appends inline content from child block if possible.
   409	                  .insertContentAt(
   410	                    state.selection.from,
   411	                    firstChildBlockHasInlineContent && blockHasInlineContent
   412	                      ? firstChildBlockContent.content
   413	                      : null,
   414	                  )
   415	                  .setTextSelection(state.selection.from)
   416	                  .scrollIntoView()
   417	                  .run()

     1	import { Node } from "prosemirror-model";
     2	import { EditorState } from "prosemirror-state";
     3	
     4	import {
     5	  BlockInfo,
     6	  getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos,
     7	} from "../../../getBlockInfoFromPos.js";
     8	
     9	/**
    10	 * Returns the block info from the parent block
    11	 * or undefined if we're at the root
    12	 */
    13	export const getParentBlockInfo = (
    14	  doc: Node,
    15	  beforePos: number,
    16	): BlockInfo | undefined => {
    17	  const $pos = doc.resolve(beforePos);
    18	  const depth = $pos.depth - 1;
    19	
    20	  if (depth < 1) {
    21	    return undefined;
    22	  }
    23	
    24	  const parentBeforePos = $pos.before(depth);
    25	  const parentNode = doc.resolve(parentBeforePos).nodeAfter;
    26	
    27	  if (!parentNode) {
    28	    return undefined;
    29	  }
    30	
    31	  if (!parentNode.type.spec.group?.includes("bnBlock")) {
    32	    return getParentBlockInfo(doc, parentBeforePos);
    33	  }
    34	
    35	  const parentBlockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos(
    36	    doc.resolve(parentBeforePos),
    37	  );
    38	
    39	  return parentBlockInfo;
    40	};
    41	
    42	/**
    43	 * Returns the block info from the sibling block before (above) the given block,
    44	 * or undefined if the given block is the first sibling.
    45	 */
    46	export const getPrevBlockInfo = (doc: Node, beforePos: number) => {
    47	  const $pos = doc.resolve(beforePos);
    48	
    49	  const indexInParent = $pos.index();
    50	
    51	  if (indexInParent === 0) {
    52	    return undefined;
    53	  }
    54	
    55	  const prevBlockBeforePos = $pos.posAtIndex(indexInParent - 1);
    56	
    57	  const prevBlockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos(
    58	    doc.resolve(prevBlockBeforePos),
    59	  );
    60	  return prevBlockInfo;
    61	};
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Returns the block info from the sibling block after (below) the given block,
    65	 * or undefined if the given block is the last sibling.
    66	 */
    67	export const getNextBlockInfo = (doc: Node, beforePos: number) => {
    68	  const $pos = doc.resolve(beforePos);
    69	
    70	  const indexInParent = $pos.index();
    71	
    72	  if (indexInParent === $pos.node().childCount - 1) {
    73	    return undefined;
    74	  }
    75	
    76	  const nextBlockBeforePos = $pos.posAtIndex(indexInParent + 1);
    77	
    78	  const nextBlockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos(
    79	    doc.resolve(nextBlockBeforePos),
    80	  );
    81	  return nextBlockInfo;
    82	};
    83	
    84	/**
    85	 * If a block has children like this:
    86	 * A
    87	 * - B
    88	 * - C
    89	 * -- D
    90	 *
    91	 * Then the bottom nested block returned is D.
    92	 */
    93	export const getBottomNestedBlockInfo = (doc: Node, blockInfo: BlockInfo) => {
    94	  while (blockInfo.childContainer) {
    95	    const group = blockInfo.childContainer.node;
    96	
    97	    const newPos = doc
    98	      .resolve(blockInfo.childContainer.beforePos + 1)
    99	      .posAtIndex(group.childCount - 1);
   100	    blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos(doc.resolve(newPos));
   101	  }
   102	
   103	  return blockInfo;
   104	};
   105	
   106	const canMerge = (prevBlockInfo: BlockInfo, nextBlockInfo: BlockInfo) => {
   107	  return (
   108	    prevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer &&
   109	    prevBlockInfo.blockContent.node.type.spec.content === "inline*" &&
   110	    prevBlockInfo.blockContent.node.childCount > 0 &&
   111	    nextBlockInfo.isBlockContainer &&
   112	    nextBlockInfo.blockContent.node.type.spec.content === "inline*"
   113	  );
   114	};
   115	
   116	const mergeBlocks = (
   117	  state: EditorState,
   118	  dispatch: ((args?: any) => any) | undefined,
   119	  prevBlockInfo: BlockInfo,
   120	  nextBlockInfo: BlockInfo,
   121	) => {
   122	  // Un-nests all children of the next block.
   123	  if (!nextBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   124	    throw new Error(
   125	      `Attempted to merge block at position ${nextBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos} into previous block at position ${prevBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos}, but next block is not a block container`,
   126	    );
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  // Removes a level of nesting all children of the next block by 1 level, if it contains both content and block
   130	  // group nodes.
   131	  if (nextBlockInfo.childContainer) {
   132	    const childBlocksStart = state.doc.resolve(
   133	      nextBlockInfo.childContainer.beforePos + 1,
   134	    );
   135	    const childBlocksEnd = state.doc.resolve(
   136	      nextBlockInfo.childContainer.afterPos - 1,
   137	    );
   138	    const childBlocksRange = childBlocksStart.blockRange(childBlocksEnd);
   139	
   140	    if (dispatch) {
   141	      const pos = state.doc.resolve(nextBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos);
   142	      state.tr.lift(childBlocksRange!, pos.depth);
   143	    }
   144	  }
   145	
   146	  // Deletes the boundary between the two blocks. Can be thought of as
   147	  // removing the closing tags of the first block and the opening tags of the
   148	  // second one to stitch them together.
   149	  if (dispatch) {
   150	    if (!prevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   151	      throw new Error(
   152	        `Attempted to merge block at position ${nextBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos} into previous block at position ${prevBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos}, but previous block is not a block container`,
   153	      );
   154	    }
   155	
   156	    // TODO: test merging between a columnList and paragraph, between two columnLists, and v.v.
   157	    dispatch(
   158	      state.tr.delete(
   159	        prevBlockInfo.blockContent.afterPos - 1,
   160	        nextBlockInfo.blockContent.beforePos + 1,
   161	      ),
   162	    );
   163	  }
   164	
   165	  return true;
   166	};
   167	
   168	export const mergeBlocksCommand =
   169	  (posBetweenBlocks: number) =>
   170	  ({
   171	    state,
   172	    dispatch,
   173	  }: {
   174	    state: EditorState;
   175	    dispatch: ((args?: any) => any) | undefined;
   176	  }) => {
   177	    const $pos = state.doc.resolve(posBetweenBlocks);
   178	    const nextBlockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos($pos);
   179	
   180	    const prevBlockInfo = getPrevBlockInfo(
   181	      state.doc,
   182	      nextBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   183	    );
   184	
   185	    if (!prevBlockInfo) {
   186	      return false;
   187	    }
   188	
   189	    const bottomNestedBlockInfo = getBottomNestedBlockInfo(
   190	      state.doc,
   191	      prevBlockInfo,
   192	    );
   193	
   194	    if (!canMerge(bottomNestedBlockInfo, nextBlockInfo)) {
   195	      return false;
   196	    }
   197	
   198	    return mergeBlocks(state, dispatch, bottomNestedBlockInfo, nextBlockInfo);
   199	  };
   200

2026-05-30 08:46:05,708 - DEBUG - swea-tools-TypeCellOS__BlockNote-2629 - Retrieved state from environment: {'working_dir': '/testbed'}
2026-05-30 08:46:05,722 - INFO - swea-agent-TypeCellOS__BlockNote-2629 - 🤖 MODEL INPUT

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /testbed/packages/core/src/api/blockManipulation/commands/moveBlocks/moveBlocks.ts:
     1	import {
     2	  NodeSelection,
     3	  Selection,
     4	  TextSelection,
     5	  Transaction,
     6	} from "prosemirror-state";
     7	import { CellSelection } from "prosemirror-tables";
     8	
     9	import { Block } from "../../../../blocks/defaultBlocks.js";
    10	import type { BlockNoteEditor } from "../../../../editor/BlockNoteEditor";
    11	import { BlockIdentifier } from "../../../../schema/index.js";
    12	import { getNearestBlockPos } from "../../../getBlockInfoFromPos.js";
    13	import { getNodeById } from "../../../nodeUtil.js";
    14	
    15	type BlockSelectionData = (
    16	  | {
    17	      type: "text";
    18	      headBlockId: string;
    19	      anchorOffset: number;
    20	      headOffset: number;
    21	    }
    22	  | {
    23	      type: "node";
    24	    }
    25	  | {
    26	      type: "cell";
    27	      anchorCellOffset: number;
    28	      headCellOffset: number;
    29	    }
    30	) & {
    31	  anchorBlockId: string;
    32	};
    33	
    34	/**
    35	 * `getBlockSelectionData` and `updateBlockSelectionFromData` are used to save
    36	 * and restore the selection within a block, when the block is moved. This is
    37	 * done by first saving the offsets of the anchor and head from the before
    38	 * positions of their surrounding blocks, as well as the IDs of those blocks. We
    39	 * can then recreate the selection by finding the blocks with those IDs, getting
    40	 * their before positions, and adding the offsets to those positions.
    41	 * @param editor The BlockNote editor instance to get the selection data from.
    42	 */
    43	function getBlockSelectionData(
    44	  editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>,
    45	): BlockSelectionData {
    46	  return editor.transact((tr) => {
    47	    const anchorBlockPosInfo = getNearestBlockPos(tr.doc, tr.selection.anchor);
    48	
    49	    if (tr.selection instanceof CellSelection) {
    50	      return {
    51	        type: "cell" as const,
    52	        anchorBlockId: anchorBlockPosInfo.node.attrs.id,
    53	        anchorCellOffset:
    54	          tr.selection.$anchorCell.pos - anchorBlockPosInfo.posBeforeNode,
    55	        headCellOffset:
    56	          tr.selection.$headCell.pos - anchorBlockPosInfo.posBeforeNode,
    57	      };
    58	    } else if (tr.selection instanceof NodeSelection) {
    59	      return {
    60	        type: "node" as const,
    61	        anchorBlockId: anchorBlockPosInfo.node.attrs.id,
    62	      };
    63	    } else {
    64	      const headBlockPosInfo = getNearestBlockPos(tr.doc, tr.selection.head);
    65	
    66	      return {
    67	        type: "text" as const,
    68	        anchorBlockId: anchorBlockPosInfo.node.attrs.id,
    69	        headBlockId: headBlockPosInfo.node.attrs.id,
    70	        anchorOffset: tr.selection.anchor - anchorBlockPosInfo.posBeforeNode,
    71	        headOffset: tr.selection.head - headBlockPosInfo.posBeforeNode,
    72	      };
    73	    }
    74	  });
    75	}
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * `getBlockSelectionData` and `updateBlockSelectionFromData` are used to save
    79	 * and restore the selection within a block, when the block is moved. This is
    80	 * done by first saving the offsets of the anchor and head from the before
    81	 * positions of their surrounding blocks, as well as the IDs of those blocks. We
    82	 * can then recreate the selection by finding the blocks with those IDs, getting
    83	 * their before positions, and adding the offsets to those positions.
    84	 * @param tr The transaction to update the selection in.
    85	 * @param data The selection data to update the selection with (generated by
    86	 * `getBlockSelectionData`).
    87	 */
    88	function updateBlockSelectionFromData(
    89	  tr: Transaction,
    90	  data: BlockSelectionData,
    91	) {
    92	  const anchorBlockPos = getNodeById(data.anchorBlockId, tr.doc)?.posBeforeNode;
    93	  if (anchorBlockPos === undefined) {
    94	    throw new Error(
    95	      `Could not find block with ID ${data.anchorBlockId} to update selection`,
    96	    );
    97	  }
    98	
    99	  let selection: Selection;
   100	  if (data.type === "cell") {
   101	    selection = CellSelection.create(
   102	      tr.doc,
   103	      anchorBlockPos + data.anchorCellOffset,
   104	      anchorBlockPos + data.headCellOffset,
   105	    );
   106	  } else if (data.type === "node") {
   107	    selection = NodeSelection.create(tr.doc, anchorBlockPos + 1);
   108	  } else {
   109	    const headBlockPos = getNodeById(data.headBlockId, tr.doc)?.posBeforeNode;
   110	    if (headBlockPos === undefined) {
   111	      throw new Error(
   112	        `Could not find block with ID ${data.headBlockId} to update selection`,
   113	      );
   114	    }
   115	
   116	    selection = TextSelection.create(
   117	      tr.doc,
   118	      anchorBlockPos + data.anchorOffset,
   119	      headBlockPos + data.headOffset,
   120	    );
   121	  }
   122	
   123	  tr.setSelection(selection);
   124	}
   125	
   126	/**
   127	 * Replaces any `columnList` blocks with the children of their columns. This is
   128	 * done here instead of in `getSelection` as we still need to remove the entire
   129	 * `columnList` node but only insert the `blockContainer` nodes inside it.
   130	 * @param blocks The blocks to flatten.
   131	 */
   132	function flattenColumns(
   133	  blocks: Block<any, any, any>[],
   134	): Block<any, any, any>[] {
   135	  return blocks
   136	    .map((block) => {
   137	      if (block.type === "columnList") {
   138	        return block.children
   139	          .map((column) => flattenColumns(column.children))
   140	          .flat();
   141	      }
   142	
   143	      return {
   144	        ...block,
   145	        children: flattenColumns(block.children),
   146	      };
   147	    })
   148	    .flat();
   149	}
   150	
   151	/**
   152	 * Removes the selected blocks from the editor, then inserts them before/after a
   153	 * reference block. Also updates the selection to match the original selection
   154	 * using `getBlockSelectionData` and `updateBlockSelectionFromData`.
   155	 * @param editor The BlockNote editor instance to move the blocks in.
   156	 * @param referenceBlock The reference block to insert the selected blocks
   157	 * before/after.
   158	 * @param placement Whether to insert the selected blocks before or after the
   159	 * reference block.
   160	 */
   161	export function moveSelectedBlocksAndSelection(
   162	  editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>,
   163	  referenceBlock: BlockIdentifier,
   164	  placement: "before" | "after",
   165	) {
   166	  // We want this to be a single step in the undo history
   167	  editor.transact((tr) => {
   168	    const blocks = editor.getSelection()?.blocks || [
   169	      editor.getTextCursorPosition().block,
   170	    ];
   171	    const selectionData = getBlockSelectionData(editor);
   172	
   173	    editor.removeBlocks(blocks);
   174	    editor.insertBlocks(flattenColumns(blocks), referenceBlock, placement);
   175	
   176	    updateBlockSelectionFromData(tr, selectionData);
   177	  });
   178	}
   179	
   180	// Checks if a block is in a valid place after being moved. This check is
   181	// primitive at the moment and only returns false if the block's parent is a
   182	// `columnList` block. This is because regular blocks cannot be direct children
   183	// of `columnList` blocks.
   184	function checkPlacementIsValid(parentBlock?: Block<any, any, any>): boolean {
   185	  return !parentBlock || parentBlock.type !== "columnList";
   186	}
   187	
   188	// Gets the placement for moving a block up. This has 3 cases:
   189	// 1. If the block has a previous sibling without children, the placement is
   190	// before it.
   191	// 2. If the block has a previous sibling with children, the placement is after
   192	// the last child.
   193	// 3. If the block has no previous sibling, but is nested, the placement is
   194	// before its parent.
   195	// If the placement is invalid, the function is called recursively until a valid
   196	// placement is found. Returns undefined if no valid placement is found, meaning
   197	// the block is already at the top of the document.
   198	function getMoveUpPlacement(
   199	  editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>,
   200	  prevBlock?: Block<any, any, any>,
   201	  parentBlock?: Block<any, any, any>,
   202	):
   203	  | { referenceBlock: BlockIdentifier; placement: "before" | "after" }
   204	  | undefined {
   205	  let referenceBlock: Block<any, any, any> | undefined;
   206	  let placement: "before" | "after" | undefined;
   207	
   208	  if (!prevBlock) {
   209	    if (parentBlock) {
   210	      referenceBlock = parentBlock;
   211	      placement = "before";
   212	    }
   213	  } else if (prevBlock.children.length > 0) {
   214	    referenceBlock = prevBlock.children[prevBlock.children.length - 1];
   215	    placement = "after";
   216	  } else {
   217	    referenceBlock = prevBlock;
   218	    placement = "before";
   219	  }
   220	
   221	  // Case when the block is already at the top of the document.
   222	  if (!referenceBlock || !placement) {
   223	    return undefined;
   224	  }
   225	
   226	  const referenceBlockParent = editor.getParentBlock(referenceBlock);
   227	  if (!checkPlacementIsValid(referenceBlockParent)) {
   228	    return getMoveUpPlacement(
   229	      editor,
   230	      placement === "after"
   231	        ? referenceBlock
   232	        : editor.getPrevBlock(referenceBlock),
   233	      referenceBlockParent,
   234	    );
   235	  }
   236	
   237	  return { referenceBlock, placement };
   238	}
   239	
   240	// Gets the placement for moving a block down. This has 3 cases:
   241	// 1. If the block has a next sibling without children, the placement is  after
   242	// it.
   243	// 2. If the block has a next sibling with children, the placement is before the
   244	// first child.
   245	// 3. If the block has no next sibling, but is nested, the placement is
   246	// after its parent.
   247	// If the placement is invalid, the function is called recursively until a valid
   248	// placement is found. Returns undefined if no valid placement is found, meaning
   249	// the block is already at the bottom of the document.
   250	function getMoveDownPlacement(
   251	  editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>,
   252	  nextBlock?: Block<any, any, any>,
   253	  parentBlock?: Block<any, any, any>,
   254	):
   255	  | { referenceBlock: BlockIdentifier; placement: "before" | "after" }
   256	  | undefined {
   257	  let referenceBlock: Block<any, any, any> | undefined;
   258	  let placement: "before" | "after" | undefined;
   259	
   260	  if (!nextBlock) {
   261	    if (parentBlock) {
   262	      referenceBlock = parentBlock;
   263	      placement = "after";
   264	    }
   265	  } else if (nextBlock.children.length > 0) {
   266	    referenceBlock = nextBlock.children[0];
   267	    placement = "before";
   268	  } else {
   269	    referenceBlock = nextBlock;
   270	    placement = "after";
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  // Case when the block is already at the bottom of the document.
   274	  if (!referenceBlock || !placement) {
   275	    return undefined;
   276	  }
   277	
   278	  const referenceBlockParent = editor.getParentBlock(referenceBlock);
   279	  if (!checkPlacementIsValid(referenceBlockParent)) {
   280	    return getMoveDownPlacement(
   281	      editor,
   282	      placement === "before"
   283	        ? referenceBlock
   284	        : editor.getNextBlock(referenceBlock),
   285	      referenceBlockParent,
   286	    );
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  return { referenceBlock, placement };
   290	}
   291	
   292	export function moveBlocksUp(editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>) {
   293	  editor.transact(() => {
   294	    const selection = editor.getSelection();
   295	    const block = selection?.blocks[0] || editor.getTextCursorPosition().block;
   296	
   297	    const moveUpPlacement = getMoveUpPlacement(
   298	      editor,
   299	      editor.getPrevBlock(block),
   300	      editor.getParentBlock(block),
   301	    );
   302	
   303	    if (!moveUpPlacement) {
   304	      return;
   305	    }
   306	
   307	    moveSelectedBlocksAndSelection(
   308	      editor,
   309	      moveUpPlacement.referenceBlock,
   310	      moveUpPlacement.placement,
   311	    );
   312	  });
   313	}
   314	
   315	export function moveBlocksDown(editor: BlockNoteEditor<any, any, any>) {
   316	  editor.transact(() => {
   317	    const selection = editor.getSelection();
   318	    const block =
   319	      selection?.blocks[selection?.blocks.length - 1] ||
   320	      editor.getTextCursorPosition().block;
   321	
   322	    const moveDownPlacement = getMoveDownPlacement(
   323	      editor,
   324	      editor.getNextBlock(block),
   325	      editor.getParentBlock(block),
   326	    );
   327	
   328	    if (!moveDownPlacement) {
   329	      return;
   330	    }
   331	
   332	    moveSelectedBlocksAndSelection(
   333	      editor,
   334	      moveDownPlacement.referenceBlock,
   335	      moveDownPlacement.placement,
   336	    );
   337	  });
   338	}

   351	
   352	    const handleDelete = () =>
   353	      this.editor.commands.first(({ chain, commands }) => [
   354	        // Deletes the selection if it's not empty.
   355	        () => commands.deleteSelection(),
   356	        // Deletes the first child block and un-nests its children, if the
   357	        // selection is empty and at the end of the current block. If both the
   358	        // parent and child blocks have inline content, the child block's
   359	        // content is appended to the parent's. The child block's own children
   360	        // are unindented before it's deleted.
   361	        () =>
   362	          commands.command(({ state }) => {
   363	            const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
   364	            if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer || !blockInfo.childContainer) {
   365	              return false;
   366	            }
   367	            const { blockContent, childContainer } = blockInfo;
   368	
   369	            const selectionAtBlockEnd =
   370	              state.selection.from === blockContent.afterPos - 1;
   371	            const selectionEmpty = state.selection.empty;
   372	
   373	            const firstChildBlockInfo = getBlockInfoFromResolvedPos(
   374	              state.doc.resolve(childContainer.beforePos + 1),
   375	            );
   376	            if (!firstChildBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
   377	              return false;
   378	            }
   379	
   380	            if (selectionAtBlockEnd && selectionEmpty) {
   381	              const firstChildBlockContent =
   382	                firstChildBlockInfo.blockContent.node;
   383	              const firstChildBlockHasInlineContent =
   384	                firstChildBlockContent.type.spec.content === "inline*";
   385	              const blockHasInlineContent =
   386	                blockContent.node.type.spec.content === "inline*";
   387	
   388	              return (
   389	                chain()
   390	                  // Un-nests child block's children if necessary.
   391	                  .insertContentAt(
   392	                    firstChildBlockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   393	                    firstChildBlockInfo.childContainer?.node.content ||
   394	                      Fragment.empty,
   395	                  )
   396	                  .deleteRange(
   397	                    // Deletes whole child container if there's only one child.
   398	                    childContainer.node.childCount === 1
   399	                      ? {
   400	                          from: childContainer.beforePos,
   401	                          to: childContainer.afterPos,
   402	                        }
   403	                      : {
   404	                          from: firstChildBlockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
   405	                          to: firstChildBlockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
   406	                        },
   407	                  )
   408	                  // Appends inline content from child block if possible.
   409	                  .insertContentAt(
   410	                    state.selection.from,
   411	                    firstChildBlockHasInlineContent && blockHasInlineContent
   412	                      ? firstChildBlockContent.content
   413	                      : null,
   414	                  )
   415	                  .setTextSelection(state.selection.from)

```typescript
// If the previous block is a columnList, moves the current block to
// the end of the last column in it.
() =>
  commands.command(({ state, tr, dispatch }) => {
    const blockInfo = getBlockInfoFromSelection(state);
    if (!blockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
      return false;
    }

    const prevBlockInfo = getPrevBlockInfo(
      state.doc,
      blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
    );
    if (!prevBlockInfo || prevBlockInfo.isBlockContainer) {
      return false;
    }

        blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos,
        blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos,
      );
      tr.insert($blockAfterPos.pos, blockInfo.bnBlock.node);

      );
```

Actually, wait. ProseMirror's auto-fitting of schema happens during `tr.replaceRangeWith` or similar operations, not during `tr.delete`. Let me check this...

Actually, ProseMirror does try to fit the schema when you use methods like `replaceWith` or `replaceRangeWith`. But `tr.delete` just deletes content. The schema is checked at the end when the transaction is applied.

So if `removeEmptyColumns` removes both columns, the columnList would have 0 children, which violates the schema. Then when the transaction is applied, ProseMirror would throw an error because the document doesn't match the schema.

```typescript
if (columnList.childCount < 2) {
    throw new Error("Invalid columnList: contains fewer than two children.");
}
```

So if both columns are empty and removed, it throws an error. But the PR description says it's a "RangeError: Position X out of range", not this error.


Wait! `$columnListPos` is resolved on the current `tr.doc`. Then it gets `$columnListPos.pos + 1` and resolves that. But `posAtIndex` is called on the ORIGINAL document resolution, not on the transaction's document. Actually, `tr.doc.resolve(...)` returns a resolved position on the current state of the transaction's document. So that should be fine.

But here's a subtle issue: The loop iterates from `columnList.childCount - 1` down to 0. But `columnList` is the node from the resolved position, which is fine. However, the `posAtIndex` call:

```typescript
const columnPos = tr.doc
  .resolve($columnListPos.pos + 1)
  .posAtIndex(columnIndex);
```

This gets the position of the column at index `columnIndex` within the columnList. But when we delete a column, the indices of the remaining columns shift! Since we're iterating backwards (from last to first), this should be fine - deleting the last column doesn't affect the indices of earlier columns.

But wait, there's a problem: `posAtIndex` is called on a resolved position inside the columnList. But the `posAtIndex` method returns the position at a given index within the parent node. If we've already deleted some columns, the subsequent calls to `posAtIndex` might return incorrect positions.

Actually, let me check: the `columnList` variable is captured at the beginning of `removeEmptyColumns`. It has the original childCount. The loop goes from last to first. When we delete a column, `tr.doc` changes, but the `resolve($columnListPos.pos + 1)` should still work because `$columnListPos.pos` is still valid (the columnList hasn't been deleted).

But `posAtIndex` on a resolved position: this resolves the position at the start of the node, then finds the position at a given child index. If we delete a child, the positions of earlier children stay the same (since we're deleting later children first). So this should be fine.

Let me think about another scenario. What if the issue is in the `fixColumnList` function when it tries to do `ReplaceAroundStep`?

```typescript
if (firstColumnEmpty) {
    tr.step(
      new ReplaceAroundStep(
        // Replaces `columnList`.
        columnListPos,
        columnListPos + columnList.nodeSize,
        // Replaces with content of last `column`.
        lastColumnAfterPos - lastColumn.nodeSize + 1,
        lastColumnAfterPos - 1,
        // Doesn't append anything.
        Slice.empty,
        0,
        false,
      ),
    );
```

Here, `columnList` is resolved at the start of the function, but by the time we get here, `removeEmptyColumns` may have already deleted some columns. So `columnList.nodeSize` might be wrong because columns were deleted!

Actually wait, let me check: `removeEmptyColumns` is called first:

```typescript
export function fixColumnList(tr: Transaction, columnListPos: number) {
  removeEmptyColumns(tr, columnListPos);

  const $columnListPos = tr.doc.resolve(columnListPos);
  const columnList = $columnListPos.nodeAfter;
  ...
```

After `removeEmptyColumns`, it re-resolves `$columnListPos` and gets `columnList` from the updated document. So `columnList` should reflect the current state with empty columns removed. That should be fine.

BUT there's another issue: `removeEmptyColumns` iterates through columns and deletes empty ones. If ALL columns are empty, they all get deleted, leaving a columnList with 0 children. Then the code at line 105-112 checks for `columnList.childCount < 2` and throws an error. But maybe in some edge case, the columnList gets 1 child (if only some columns were empty and got removed, but the schema requires at least 2, so ProseMirror might add empty ones automatically?).

Wait, actually, I think the issue might be something else entirely. Let me look at the `removeEmptyColumns` function more carefully:

```typescript
for (
    let columnIndex = columnList.childCount - 1;
    columnIndex >= 0;
    columnIndex--
) {
    const columnPos = tr.doc
      .resolve($columnListPos.pos + 1)
      .posAtIndex(columnIndex);
```

`$columnListPos.pos + 1` is the position right after the opening tag of the columnList. If columns are being deleted, does this position still correctly resolve? Let me think...

When we delete a column, the columnList's content shrinks. But `$columnListPos.pos + 1` is still the position after the opening tag. `posAtIndex` on this should still work because the resolved position refers to the parent node and returns the position of the nth child.

Actually, there's a subtle bug: `posAtIndex` returns the position at the start of the nth child. But after deleting some children (iterating backwards), the position of the remaining children doesn't change. However, `$columnListPos.pos` could become invalid if the columnList itself was deleted or moved. But that shouldn't happen because we're only deleting columns inside it.

Let me think about this differently. Let me actually try to reproduce the bug. Let me look at existing tests to understand the structure

     9	const getEditor = setupTestEnv();
    10	
    11	function replaceBlocks(
    12	  editor: BlockNoteEditor,
    13	  blocksToRemove: BlockIdentifier[],
    14	  blocksToInsert: PartialBlock<any, any, any>[],
    15	) {
    16	  return editor.transact((tr) =>
    17	    removeAndInsertBlocks(tr, blocksToRemove, blocksToInsert),
    18	  );
    19	}

2026-05-30 08:48:26,105 - DEBUG - swea-lm-TypeCellOS__BlockNote-2629 - n_cache_control: 2
2026-05-30 08:48:39,214 - DEBUG - swea-lm-TypeCellOS__BlockNote-2629 - Response: ModelResponse(id='7ffa9d8de7e14385b8607e08f9dca56d', created=1780130907, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='tool_calls', index=0, message=Message(content='Now let me think about the actual bug. The PR description says:\n\n1. Insert a multi-column block (e.g., 3 columns)\n2. Move cursor to the next block (below the columns)\n3. Type some text (e.g., aaa)\n4. Press and hold Backspace to delete continuously\n\nThis results in "RangeError: Position X out of range".\n\nLet me trace through what happens when you hold backspace:\n\n1. First few backspaces: delete characters from "aaa" -> "aa" -> "a" -> ""\n2. When the paragraph is empty, the next backspace triggers the "Deletes the current block if it\'s an empty block with inline content" handler (line 213).\n3. This handler calls `chain().deleteRange({ from: blockInfo.bnBlock.beforePos, to: blockInfo.bnBlock.afterPos })` which calls `removeBlocks` with that block\'s ID.\n4. `removeAndInsertBlocks` is called, which deletes the block. But it also collects `columnListPositions` and calls `fixColumnList`.\n5. Now if we have a scenario where the deleted block was after a columnList, the columnList might be untouched. So `fixColumnList` wouldn\'t be called (no column or columnList was involved).\n\nBut wait, what if the block being deleted is inside a column? Let me trace that:\n\nActually, re-reading the PR description: "Insert a multi-column block (e.g., 3 columns), Move cursor to the next block (below the columns), Type some text (e.g., aaa), Press and hold Backspace to delete continuously"\n\nSo the block AFTER the multi-column block is being deleted character by character until it\'s empty, then the block itself is deleted. That shouldn\'t involve columns at all.\n\nBut maybe the issue is about what happens AFTER the block is deleted? The empty paragraph after the columnList gets deleted, and then... the cursor ends up somewhere, and then pressing backspace again might trigger something involving the columns.\n\nLet me look at the `getPrevBlockInfo` function.
